title: Phrases: connect words into sentences
description: Move from isolated words to full phrases — your first real sentences
text: the dog and the cat had a sad day they tried hard to win at last and were happy

tips.text: Take the sentence in with your eyes before your hands move. Reading one letter at a time is how you lose the thread; read the line, then set it down.
The space between words is part of the music, not a stop. Glide into it without pausing and the sentence holds together.

tips.target: the dog and the cat had a sad day

tips.text: Steady beats fast. A typist who rushes and stops will always lose to one who keeps an even pace.

tips.target: they tried hard to win at last and were happy

character_tips.anna: These are real sentences now, not drills. Feel how the rhythm carries you from one word to the next. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.maxim: Let your eyes run a word or two ahead of your hands. The fingers do their best work when they already know what is coming. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.knopych: Words joined into sentences. That is the whole trade in miniature. Keep the line moving. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
character_tips.klavochka: You are putting words together into something whole. That is writing, plainly. Stay with the rhythm. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
